This Rapid Cloud Assessment provides customer with an independent, non-intrusive evaluation of their cloud security posture across AWS, Azure, GCP. FortiCNAPP connects using read-only API access — no agents, no software to deploy, no disruption to running workloads — and analyzes data entirely within the organization's own cloud environment. The findings in this report cover five prioritized risk domains: critical compliance failures, high-risk vulnerabilities, identity and entitlement gaps, and active behavioral alerts. Each finding is accompanied by business context and a recommended remediation action.

Discovered Risk Findings

02 — Compliance
Critical Non-Compliance
High-risk control failures affecting governance and cloud exposure.
03 — Vulnerabilities

Admin Access & Entitlements
Overprivileged identities, dormant admins, and MFA gaps.
05 — Alerts
Critical Alerts
Active detections requiring rapid triage or containment.

Executive Summary

FortiCNAPP has completed a comprehensive cloud security assessment of customer's environment spanning cloud misconfigurations, workload vulnerabilities, identity and entitlement risk, and behavioral alert signals. The findings presented in this report represent the highest-priority security issues observed during the assessment period and require leadership attention to prevent potential business disruption, data loss, or regulatory exposure.

Across cloud environments, FortiCNAPP identified 2 critical non-compliance finding(s) against industry benchmarks (CIS). These misconfigurations represent gaps in foundational controls — including access management, encryption, network exposure, and logging — that an attacker could exploit without any specialized tooling.

Vulnerability analysis flagged 8 host(s) and/or container image(s) with critical-severity CVEs and a FortiCNAPP Risk Score of 9 or higher. These represent the most dangerous unpatched conditions in the environment, where exploitation could result in full host compromise or lateral movement to adjacent workloads.

Identity and entitlement analysis identified 59 admin identity(ies) with full administrative privileges and 167 identity(ies) with excessive unused entitlements. Overprivileged accounts dramatically increase the blast radius of any credential compromise and represent a persistent insider threat risk.

FortiCNAPP generated 4 critical behavioral alert(s) during the assessment window. These alerts represent correlated signals of anomalous activity — including suspicious logins, privilege escalation, and audit log tampering — that may indicate active or attempted compromise requiring immediate investigation.

1 ip-172-31-18-225.ca-central-1.compute.internal AWS home/ubuntu/.ssh/samv_ssh.pem ssh-rsa Key/cert file — 600 (owner read-only); never world or group readable Upgrade to ssh-ed25519 — stronger, faster, and immune to RSA timing attacks

7 FortiNac-node1 Unknown root/.ssh/id_rsa ssh-rsa Linux/Unix — verify 600 (owner read-only); audit with: stat -c "%a %U %G" Upgrade to ssh-ed25519 — stronger, faster, and immune to RSA timing attacks
▲ Risk Summary — Secrets Posture

✓ No risk exposure. All 8 detected secret(s) reside in chmod-600 protected locations (owner read-only). These files are accessible only by the owning process or root — they do not constitute an active risk finding. Continue enforcing strict file permissions and rotate keys on a scheduled basis as part of standard security hygiene.

⚠ Key Algorithm Advisory: 7 key(s) use the legacy ssh-rsa algorithm. Even though the files are properly protected, generate replacements using ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -o -a 100 (rsa-sha2-256) or migrate to ssh-ed25519 (ssh-keygen -t ed25519) — RSA is susceptible to timing attacks regardless of file permissions.

▲ Risk Summary — Why Immediate Action Matters

2 critical compliance finding(s) were identified across cloud environments. These are not theoretical risks — CIS-critical controls represent the subset of misconfigurations most frequently exploited in pre-breach cloud environments. Unresolved critical findings leave foundational security controls — access management, encryption, network exposure, and audit logging — in a failed state. Each open finding must be treated as an active attack vector until the underlying control is remediated and verified by a rescan.

FortiCNAPP Risk Score Explained

The Risk Score (1–10) is a composite metric calculated by FortiCNAPP that weighs CVSS severity, number of affected hosts/images, package blast radius, and the presence of public exploit code. A score of 9.0+ places a vulnerability in the top tier requiring immediate attention regardless of CVSS alone.

▲ Risk Summary — Why Immediate Action Matters

8 host(s) and/or container(s) are running with unpatched CVEs at FortiCNAPP Risk Score 9 or higher. This composite score accounts for CVSS severity, the number of affected assets, blast radius, and the presence of public exploit code — meaning real-world attack tooling is available and actively used against these vulnerabilities. The average time to exploit a disclosed CVE is 12 days. For every day patching is deferred in this tier, the probability of a successful, in-the-wild exploitation event increases measurably.

Identity Risk Commentary

80% of breaches in the public cloud are caused by stolen or compromised identities — making identity the #1 attack vector in cloud environments (CrowdStrike Global Threat Report). FortiCNAPP's CIEM analysis reveals a pattern of persistent over-privilege across cloud identities. Admin accounts with full administrative rights and no MFA represent the single highest-risk condition in the environment — a single successful phishing attack on any such account would grant an adversary unrestricted access to cloud resources, billing controls, and data stores.

Standing privilege — permanent admin access that is always active rather than granted on demand — violates the principle of least privilege and is a recognized compliance failure under CIS, NIST, and most regulatory frameworks. The right-sizing of these entitlements should be treated as a compliance requirement, not a best-effort improvement.

Dormant and unused entitlements represent an invisible attack surface. Identities that have not used the majority of their granted permissions in the past 90 days should be reduced to their observed access pattern. FortiCNAPP can generate right-sized permission recommendations to accelerate this remediation.

▲ Risk Summary — Why Immediate Action Matters

59 admin identity(ies) with full cloud privileges have MFA disabled. A single phishing email targeting any one of these accounts grants an attacker unrestricted access to every cloud resource — compute, storage, networking, and billing controls. Admin accounts without MFA are the leading vector for cloud account takeover. Enforcing MFA on these accounts alone reduces account compromise risk by 99.9% against automated and credential-based attacks. This is the single highest-priority remediation in this report.

5. Top Critical Alerts

The following FortiCNAPP critical-severity alerts were generated during the assessment window. Each alert represents a detection from FortiCNAPP's behavioral analytics engine — triggered when observed cloud activity deviates significantly from the established baseline. Composite alerts correlate multiple signals and indicate a higher likelihood of active attack activity.

▲ Risk Summary — Why Immediate Action Matters

4 critical behavioral alert(s) were generated during the assessment window. FortiCNAPP critical alerts represent the highest-confidence detections — triggered only when observed activity significantly deviates from a learned behavioral baseline, or when multiple correlated signals indicate coordinated attack activity. Each unresolved alert extends attacker dwell time: the industry average is 194 days from initial compromise to detection. Every day without investigation increases both the scope of compromise and the cost of incident response. Treat each unresolved critical alert as an active incident until proven otherwise.

Fortinet Product and Solution Recommendations

The FortiCNAPP non-intrusive assessment of customer reveals a multi-layered risk exposure spanning cloud configuration, workload security, identity governance, and behavioral threat signals. 2 critical compliance finding(s) confirm foundational control gaps — including access management, network exposure, and audit logging — that represent exploitable attack surface without any specialized tooling.8 host(s) and/or container(s) carry unpatched CVEs with a Risk Score of 9 or higher, where known exploit code is publicly available and active exploitation in the wild has been documented.59 admin identity(ies) lack MFA, presenting a direct path to full environment compromise through a single credential phishing event.4 critical behavioral alert(s) indicate active or attempted anomalous activity requiring immediate investigation.

The following Fortinet products are recommended to address the specific risk vectors identified above. FortiCNAPP provides the continuous agentless detection layer; the complementary products below add prevention, enforcement, and response capabilities to convert visibility into active protection.

FortiCNAPP
Cloud Visibility
CSPMCWPPCIEMThreat Detection
  • CSPM — Continuous misconfiguration detection across AWS, Azure, and GCP against CIS, PCI-DSS, and SOC2 benchmarks with automated drift alerting.
  • CWPP — Agentless vulnerability and runtime threat detection for hosts, containers, and serverless workloads with FortiCNAPP Risk Scoring.
  • CIEM — Cloud Identity & Entitlement Management: right-size permissions, eliminate standing privilege, and enforce MFA across all cloud identities.
FortiGate
Cloud Edge Security
Virtual PatchingNetwork ControlsZTNA
  • Cloud Edge Firewall — North-South traffic inspection and IPS-based virtual patching at cloud ingress/egress for immediate CVE coverage without maintenance windows.
  • East-West Microsegmentation — enforce zero-trust workload connectivity to contain lateral movement and limit blast radius from compromised instances.
  • ZTNA & SD-WAN — replace legacy VPN with identity-aware, least-privilege access to cloud resources for admin, developer, and remote sessions.
FortiAppSec Cloud
Cloud Application Web & API Security · SaaS · DDoS CDN WAAP · ALB
WAAP DDoS API Protection CDN
  • WAAP at the CDN Edge — globally distributed Web Application & API Protection with volumetric and application-layer DDoS scrubbing before traffic reaches the origin.
  • API Discovery & Protection — auto-discover shadow and undocumented APIs, enforce schema-based policies, and block abuse and bot-driven attacks.
  • ALB-Native Deployment — inline security for traffic flowing through Application Load Balancers with no agent overhead and full SSL/TLS visibility.
FortiWeb
Cloud Application Web & API · Gen AI (MCP) · ALB
Virtual Patching WAF Gen AI Security ALB
  • WAF + Virtual Patching — ML-driven web attack prevention with immediate CVE exploit coverage for web-facing stacks before the underlying package is patched.
  • Gen AI & MCP Security — protect AI inference APIs and Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoints from prompt injection, data exfiltration, and API abuse.
  • ALB Integration — transparent inline inspection of HTTP/S traffic behind Application Load Balancers with API gateway schema validation and rate limiting.
FortiCASB-SSPM
Data Governance & Shadow IT · SaaS Supply Chain · SaaS Compliance
Identity RiskSaaS Compliance Shadow IT Data Governance Supply Chain
  • SaaS Security Posture Management — continuous misconfiguration detection and auto-remediation across M365, Google Workspace, Salesforce, and other sanctioned SaaS platforms.
  • Shadow IT & SaaS Supply Chain Risk — discover unsanctioned applications, assess OAuth and third-party integration risk, and enforce SaaS access governance policies.
  • Data Governance & DLP — classify and protect sensitive data in motion across SaaS platforms with policy-driven enforcement and audit-ready compliance reporting.
